title: DIY Hat Rack
summary: This DIY wall-mounted hat rack is a great beginner woodworking project and will help organize any entryway, mudroom, or closet!
description: This DIY wall-mounted hat rack is a great beginner woodworking project and will help organize any entryway, mudroom, or closet! Feel free to modify the dimensions depending on your storage needs.

steps.title: Cut Your Wood
steps.body: Cut your wood to the specified dimensions.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Drill 3 pocket holes along one end of your 8 inch long 1x10 pieces. If you want to add the optional hooks at the bottom of your hat rack, then also add 3 pocket holes along the parallel end of one of those 8 inch long boards.

steps.title: Attach Shelves
steps.body: Use 1 1/4" long pocket hole screws, attach your shelves. Start by attaching one of your 8" long boards flush to the top edge of your long 1x10 board, then measure and mark 6" below that to add your next shelf. Repeat until each shelf has been attached, each of them 6" apart.

steps.title: Add Bottom Piece
steps.body: Use 1 3/4" pocket hole screws to attach your 1x3 board to the front of the bottom shelf. (Optional.) This will allow you to add hooks for additional storage for things like tote bags, umbrellas, keys, etc.

steps.title: Stain or Paint
steps.body: Once your hat rack has been assembled, paint or stain it in the color of your choice. I used an Early American stain for this one.

steps.title: Add Hanging Hardware
steps.body: Attach a keyhole bracket to the back, then hang it on the wall using a wall anchor and screw.
